sources.name: HESA / Student Minds
sources.dataset: Student mental health disclosure rates and University Mental Health Survey

sources.name: Office for Students
sources.dataset: Mental health in higher education: insights from data

metadata.methodology: Disclosure rates represent students declaring a mental health condition to their university at enrolment or during their studies, from HESA student record data. Counselling demand index is indexed to 2015=100, based on AMOSSHE survey data on counselling session requests vs capacity across UK universities. Suspected suicide figures are from University Mental Health Advisors Network reports.
knownIssues: Disclosure rates reflect students who actively declare a condition; actual prevalence of mental health difficulties is significantly higher based on survey evidence
Counselling demand data relies on university self-reporting to AMOSSHE; not all universities participate and smaller institutions are under-represented
Student suicide data is based on suspected cases and subject to coroner findings; the actual number confirmed as suicide will differ from provisional reports
